interpretation: The Gandhian symbolic order achieves moderate-high symbolic strength through a theoretically novel route: rather than sacralizing national identity through sacred-enemy construction or blood-sacrifice demands, Gandhi's doctrine redirects sacrifice entirely to self-suffering. Body-force (violence against the enemy) is explicitly rejected as inferior to soul-force (accepting penalty for truth-bearing resistance). This anti-sacrificial logic gives the symbolic order high corrigibility — the movement cannot generate internal purges or sacrificial escalation from its own constitutive principles — while achieving independence through collective action. The clearest theoretical countercase to the pathological sacrificial-escalation gold cases.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Chain thin; theoretical founding texts only. Operational episodes and partition violence complicate the score.

mechanismSummary: Gandhi's satyagraha doctrine explicitly substitutes self-suffering for enemy-directed violence, achieving political transformation through soul-force rather than body-force — a constitutive anti-sacrificial restraint that structurally prevents sacred-enemy escalation.
